task_name: Write permit enforcement stage 3B - byte output and create/update guard
goal: Wire one-shot write permit fields through the Bluepilot builder write path without performing any live write. The final UTF-8 content bytes must be hashed immediately before corridor assessment, the corridor must receive permit coordinates, and the actual GitHub contents PUT must obey explicit create-only or update-only semantics derived from the permit.
scope_out: Do not change maya-core from this repo.
Do not add or change runtime endpoints, package scripts, dependencies, or tools.
Do not perform a live GitHub write, call live maya-core, or alter Render environment variables.
Do not implement the live runbook or admin permit issuing path; that is stage 3C.
Do not add new Opus-branded function, feature, or task names.
claims: CorridorAssessInput accepts optional one-shot permit fields: permitId, repo, branch, path, op, baseSha, contentHash, and contentLen, and forwards them unchanged to maya-core.
putFileContent keeps the existing no-mode behavior unchanged, but also accepts an optional explicit mode for create-only or update-only writes.
For create-only, putFileContent sends a PUT without sha and does not silently convert to update if the file exists.
For update-only, putFileContent sends exactly expectedBaseSha as sha and does not inspect GitHub for a replacement sha.

The permit-aware direct whole-file path computes contentHash from the final UTF-8 content string before base64 encoding, sends the permit coordinates to assessCorridor, and writes only after the corridor allows with reason permit_consumed.
If the permit corridor assessment is rejected, the GitHub write function is not called.
Existing non-permit behavior remains unchanged, including default-target /push behavior and non-default direct whole-file behavior without permitId.
assumptions: maya-core BP-144 stage 3A is deployed separately and understands the permit fields.
builder/src/writePermitContentHash.ts is already present from BP-144 stage 1 and must not be modified here.
The first live stage 3C write will use op=create against the sandbox; update support is still implemented and tested to close the TOCTOU class.
Patch-job permit enforcement is intentionally not broadened in this task unless the final bytes are available at the same byte-output boundary.
dod: builder/tests/mayaBuilderGateClient.test.ts proves permit fields are serialized to the corridor endpoint.
builder/tests/opusPatchMode.test.ts proves create-only, update-only, and existing no-mode behavior.
builder/tests/opusSmartPush.test.ts proves permit-consumed allows one direct write, corridor rejection prevents writes, and contentHash/contentLen match final UTF-8 content.
npm run typecheck in builder/ passes.
No live write, package change, tool change, server route change, or maya-core change occurs.
stop_conditions: The final UTF-8 content is not available before the corridor check.
The permit path would need a live write or live maya-core call to verify.
A package or dependency change becomes necessary.
The update-only path cannot pass expectedBaseSha directly to GitHub.
Patch-job permit enforcement would require guessing final bytes.
prior_task_findings: BP-144 stage 3A is complete in maya-core at 758ab63: the corridor can require and consume permits server-side. Claude's stage 3B design was checked against local Bluepilot code: putFileContent currently performs GET then PUT and silently selects create or update from current GitHub state; the permit path must instead bind op=create/update and hash the final UTF-8 content before base64.
